forensic_flags.narrative: DPO ~113 days (FY2025) vs a ~30-60 day norm — consistent with supplier finance booked in trade payables. Included in economic debt — behaves like a callable facility.
forensic_flags.pointer: AP footnote; supplier-finance disclosure (ASU 2022-04, ASC 405-50); MD&A liquidity.

forensic_flags.narrative: Cumulative proxy EBITDA $11,829M converted to 59% in OCF ($7,004M). Covenants are EBITDA-based; default is cash-based.
forensic_flags.pointer: CF working-capital lines; receivables securitization / transfer-of-financial-assets footnotes; revenue-recognition policy.

forensic_flags.narrative: Free cash flow -$119M in FY2025 (OCF − capex). Cash covers ~102 months at this burn.
forensic_flags.pointer: MD&A liquidity & capital resources; revolver availability / borrowing base; maturity schedule.

covenants.creditor_note: Agents/trustee as named in the agreement. Beneficial holders of loans and bonds are not public; registered-fund holdings (N-PORT) are a partial view shown separately when available.
covenants.guarantors: Each of the Guarantors party to the Agreement (the Loan Parties); the Agreement contemplates additional guarantors under Section 6.16. The ABS Loan Parties / ABS InfraCos are structured as separate entities under the ABS (receivables) facility.

financial_covenants.kind: Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio test (governs 'Amortization Period')
financial_covenants.test_frequency: Tested on a rolling basis; an Amortization Period commences on the first date the ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 and ends when it no longer exceeds that level
financial_covenants.springing_trigger: Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeding 2.50:1.00
financial_covenants.quote: “Amortization Period” shall mean each period commencing on the first date on which the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 and ending on the first date thereafter when the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio on such date does not exceed 2.50:1.00.

baskets.name: Asset Disposition de minimis basket (general disposition of Capital Stock/property/assets)
baskets.value: $5 million per calendar year in the aggregate
baskets.quote: any sale, lease, transfer, issuance or other disposition ... in a single transaction or series of related transactions with a fair market value ... not to exceed $5 million per calendar year in the aggregate

baskets.name: Material Indebtedness threshold (cross-default reference)
baskets.quote: “Material Indebtedness” shall mean any Indebtedness (other than the Obligations) of the Loan Parties or any of their Subsidiaries in an aggregate principal amount exceeding $5 million.

baskets.name: Material Contract threshold
baskets.quote: any contract or agreement, or series of related agreements ... that involves consideration in the aggregate, of more than $5 million per annum.

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The excerpt does not include an unrestricted-subsidiary designation mechanic in the ordinary covenant-lite sense. Instead, 'Designation' refers to designating the Loan Parties as 'unrestricted subsidiaries' under the Existing Debt Documents, and the deal contemplates a separate ring-fenced ABS/receivables structure (ABS Borrower, ABS Holdings, ABS InfraCos, ABS Loan Parties). Section 6.01 (Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises) and Section 6.17 (Delaware LLC Divisions) further constrain structural transfers. Full designation flexibility cannot be confirmed from the provided text.
covenants.anchor_clause: Section 6.01. Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises. (The table of contents identifies Section 6.01 as an express covenant prohibiting liability management exercises — a distressed analyst must read this bespoke anti-LME clause first, as it directly governs uptier/dropdown capacity; the operative text was not included in the excerpt provided.)

lme_vectors.rationale: The agreement contains a bespoke Section 6.01 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises' plus intercreditor machinery, but the operative text was not in the excerpt, so the precise scope of uptier/priming protection cannot be confirmed.
lme_vectors.basis: TOC Section 6.01 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises'; Section 6.13 Intercreditor Agreements; defined 'Junior Debt' and 'Pari Passu Debt' concepts.
lme_vectors.quote: Section 6.01. Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises

lme_vectors.rationale: The deal is built around a segregated ABS/receivables structure (ABS Borrower, ABS InfraCos, IP License / Trademark License arrangements with CSC) and includes an anti-LME covenant and Delaware LLC division restriction, suggesting attention to asset/IP transfer, but the governing transfer-restriction text is not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Defined ABS Transaction Parties, IP License Agreement, Trademark License Agreement; TOC Section 6.01 (anti-LME) and Section 6.17 (Delaware LLC Divisions); definition of 'Material Assets' and 'Material Intellectual Property'.
lme_vectors.quote: “Material Assets” shall mean any property or assets (whether intellectual property or otherwise) material to the business or operations of the Loan Parties (taken as a whole).

lme_vectors.rationale: The provided excerpt (definitions and TOC) does not include the operative Section 6.04 incremental/ratio debt capacity, MFN, or free-and-clear amount, so incremental debt capacity cannot be assessed from the text.

lme_vectors.quote: Section 6.04. Limitation on Indebtedness

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 6.05 (Limitation on Restricted Payments) is referenced but the operative RP capacity, builder basket and carve-outs are not included in the excerpt, so RP leakage capacity cannot be assessed.

lme_vectors.quote: any Restricted Payment that is permitted to be made under Section 6.05, any transaction specifically excluded from the definition of Restricted Payment and the making of any Permitted Payment and Permitted Investment

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The agreement is structured around a fixed set of Loan Parties/Subsidiaries; the only 'Designation' concept in the excerpted text refers to the designation of the Loan Parties as 'unrestricted subsidiaries' under the Existing Debt Documents (i.e., these borrowers sit outside the CSC/Existing Debt restricted group), not a general covenant power for the Borrowers to designate their own unrestricted subsidiaries. No standard unrestricted-subsidiary designation basket appears in the provided text.
covenants.anchor_clause: Section 6.01. Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises (as listed in the Table of Contents and reinforced by the covenant package). This is the single clause a distressed analyst must read because it directly addresses LME capacity; the excerpted text confirms its existence but does not reproduce its operative language.

lme_vectors.rationale: The agreement contemplates Pari Passu Debt, Junior Debt and multiple intercreditor agreements, and a general 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises' (Section 6.01) is present, but the operative sacred-rights/priming language is not reproduced in the excerpt so the degree of protection against a non-pro-rata uptier cannot be confirmed.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 6.01 titled 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises'; definitions of Junior Debt, Pari Passu Debt and Additional Intercreditor Agreements (Equal Priority and First Lien/Second Lien forms) are provided.

lme_vectors.rationale: A 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises' covenant exists and the structure ring-fences ABS/IP via a dedicated IP License Agreement and Shared Services Agreement with amendment restrictions (Section 6.15), constraining transfers of material IP/assets outside the group.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 6.01 Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises; IP License Agreement defined; Section 6.15 Amendments to the Shared Services Agreement; asset-disposition definition carves IP licensing to ordinary course and Section 6.08 governs dispositions.
lme_vectors.quote: “IP License Agreement” shall mean that certain Intellectual Property License Agreement ... as the same may be amended ... in accordance with the provisions hereof (including Section 6.15 hereof).

lme_vectors.rationale: The deal includes a $1,100 million Incremental Term Loan facility and a general limitation on indebtedness (Section 6.04) with a ratio construct (Combined Net Leverage Ratio referencing Section 6.04(b)), but the incremental capacity, MFN and ratio thresholds are not set out in the excerpted text.
lme_vectors.basis: $1,100 million Incremental Term Loan Commitment; Section 6.04 Limitation on Indebtedness; Combined Net Leverage Ratio definition referencing debt incurred under Section 6.04(b).
lme_vectors.quote: the pro forma calculation of the Combined Net Leverage Ratio shall not give effect to (i) any Indebtedness incurred on the date of determination pursuant to Section 6.04(b)

lme_vectors.rationale: A dedicated Limitation on Restricted Payments (Section 6.05) exists and RP/Permitted Payment/Permitted Investment concepts are referenced, but the RP builder basket and fixed baskets are not reproduced in the excerpted text so leakage capacity cannot be quantified.

financial_covenants.quote: "Amortization Period" shall mean each period commencing on the first date on which the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 and ending on the first date thereafter when the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio on such date does not exceed 2.50:1.00.

baskets.name: Asset Disposition general per-year basket
baskets.value: $5 million per calendar year in the aggregate
baskets.quote: any sale, lease, transfer, issuance or other disposition... of Capital Stock, properties or assets in a single transaction or series of related transactions with a fair market value... not to exceed $5 million per calendar year in the aggregate

baskets.name: General/ordinary-course Investments basket (Permitted Investment clause (q))
baskets.quote: Investments in the ordinary course of business... in an aggregate amount at the time of such Investment not to exceed $5 million plus the amount of any distributions, dividends, payments or other returns in respect of such Investments

baskets.name: Material Indebtedness / Material Contract threshold
baskets.quote: any Indebtedness (other than the Obligations) of the Loan Parties or any of their Subsidiaries in an aggregate principal amount exceeding $5 million

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Unusual structure: the Loan Parties here are themselves entities that were "designated" as unrestricted subsidiaries under the Existing Debt Documents (see "Designation"). The agreement contains a dedicated Section 6.01 "Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises" and Section 6.15 restricting amendments to the Shared Services Agreement / IP License Agreement, indicating tight control over subsidiary/asset movements. Text provided does not include a general unrestricted-subsidiary designation mechanic within this facility.

covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The indenture contemplates Unrestricted Subsidiaries and permits dispositions of their Capital Stock/Indebtedness/securities free of the Asset Disposition regime (Asset Disposition definition clause 15). Consolidated Net Income excludes income of non-Restricted Subsidiaries except to the extent of cash actually distributed, and Restricted Payments capacity (Section 4.05) governs designation value leakage. Full designation conditions were not included in the excerpt provided.
covenants.anchor_clause: "Limitation on Transfer of Assets by Restricted Subsidiaries." (Section 4.24) — read alongside Section 4.05 (Limitation on Restricted Payments) and the Unrestricted Subsidiary designation mechanics, as these govern the ability to move value beyond the guarantor group.
